title: One Mailbag After Another, and the Updated PTA Movie Rankings
format: A listener mailbag on 'One Battle After Another' and Paul Thomas Anderson, updated rankings of all ten of his films, a viewing syllabus, and visits from Adam Nayman and Andy Greenwald.

segments.heading: The ‘One Battle After Another’ box office

films.title: One Battle After Another
films.note: $22.4M domestic, $48.5M global, easily PTA's biggest opening. Sean argues the box-office scolds should relax: the movie is an anomaly born of Mike DeLuca and the TCM-Zaslav détente, and a movie like this becomes valuable over decades, not one weekend.

films.title: There Will Be Blood
films.note: PTA's prior high-water mark at $77M; Sean expects One Battle to sail past it.

films.title: Sinners
films.note: The recent example of studio money on original work paying off big.

films.title: Killers of the Flower Moon
films.note: Van Lathan's grievance: called a 'failure' in a way superhero flops never are.

films.title: Aquaman
films.note: Sean's 'deadstock' contrast: a billion-dollar hit that curdles in value as DC resets, versus a movie that lasts.

films.title: The Ten Commandments
films.note: The 4K on sale that got Sean thinking about a 'forever movie' the studio still monetizes 70 years on.

films.title: Black Adam
films.note: 'This is not like Black Adam failed.' A different circumstance entirely.

films.title: Witness
films.note: Collateral damage in the disc-hoarding bit; Sean owns it but rates it good, not great.

films.title: One Battle After Another
films.note: The through-line of the whole bag: favorite gags (Benicio's dance, 'semen demon,' 'Life, man'), VistaVision vs. IMAX vs. 70mm (Sean's Vista print snapped 40 minutes in), Halloween-costume potential, the hawk-to-Black-Hawk cut, the Steely Dan karate scene, and the closing letter montage.

films.title: Punch-Drunk Love
films.note: Olivia's question (what makes a PTA movie a PTA movie) pivots on how the same guy made this and One Battle: socially obtuse men, a screamer on the other end of the phone, John Brion's big-and-nervy score.

films.title: Eddington
films.note: The most-asked comparison. Sean's read: Eddington is about being on your phone and has no hope; One Battle is about being in the world and lets itself believe in some. Mirror images: one comic and devastating, one sincere and funny.

films.title: Star Wars
films.note: Peter's question and Leo's own observation: revolutionaries betrayed by the chosen one, a child told a lie about a dead parent.

films.title: Star Wars: The Last Jedi
films.note: Came up with Leo re: how hard it is to be an aging, grouchy revolutionary, Mark Hamill in the Rian Johnson film.

films.title: Goodfellas
films.note: The convenience-store wig-blown-off shot, widely noted as very Scorsese.

segments.heading: The great moviegoing events
segments.note: John's question — when did a movie last feel this monumental

films.title: Pulp Fiction
films.note: One of Sean's 1994 trio (with Forrest Gump and The Lion King) that felt huge to three different audiences at once.

films.title: Forrest Gump
films.note: The parents' pick of that year; 'boomer crap or not,' it was massive.

films.title: The Lion King
films.note: The kids' engine of the '94 everything-at-once moment.

films.title: Titanic
films.note: Sean's clearest precedent: declared a disaster pre-release, then the biggest thing in movies for years, Swifties energy for a historical drama, and for Leo.

films.title: The Matrix
films.note: Out-of-nowhere game-changer, though Sean isn't sure anyone called it an instant all-timer at the time.

films.title: The Lord of the Rings: The Fellowship of the Ring
films.note: The trilogy as a massive event for movie fans; Amanda keeps hearing about it.

films.title: The Dark Knight
films.note: A genuine event; Sean rates it bigger than Inception.

films.title: Inception
films.note: The Nolan follow-up event, noted as 'its own thing.'

films.title: Avatar
films.note: Cameron doing spectacle-as-event again.

films.title: Mad Max: Fury Road
films.note: 'Witness me' entered the vocabulary; peers located it as something special.

films.title: Get Out
films.note: Confirmed a classic within six months, its phrases and ideas got into the culture fast.

films.title: Avengers: Infinity War
films.note: Part of the big-event Marvel run John invokes.

films.title: Avengers: Endgame
films.note: People filming the screen and losing their minds: the last theater-mania event before Barbenheimer.

films.title: Barbie
films.note: Half of Barbenheimer: 300 people in pink in the room.

films.title: Oppenheimer
films.note: The other half; a billion-dollar Oscar steamroller that, unlike One Battle, drew more early detractors.

films.title: Parasite
films.note: Jack's grown-up example: 'holy shit, what just happened' at an Ithaca film-school screening.

films.title: Once Upon a Time in Hollywood
films.note: The ArcLight Dome screening in the Tarantino event vein; also the Leo pointing-at-the-screen meme.

films.title: Inglourious Basterds
films.note: Sean's Tarantino moment: the palpable tension of the opening farmhouse scene.

films.title: Clueless
films.note: A huge one for Amanda personally, if not on the Oppenheimer scale.

films.title: Lady Bird
films.note: Another personal-scale event for Amanda.

segments.heading: The updated PTA movie rankings
segments.note: Sean's order; Amanda's noted per film
segments.subject: Paul Thomas Anderson

films.title: There Will Be Blood
films.note: Sean's No. 1, unmoved for five years: 'I felt my soul leave my body.' Amanda has it 4th.

films.title: The Master
films.note: Sean's No. 2. Amanda's No. 3, and the film she still can't fully explain her feelings about.

films.title: One Battle After Another
films.note: Sean's No. 3 ('too soon to know'); Amanda's No. 2. Both concede it may still climb.

films.title: Phantom Thread
films.note: Sean's No. 4, 'a great film about marriage.' Amanda's No. 1, the shared top of neither list but hers.

films.title: Magnolia
films.note: No. 5 for both. Sean's had a full turnaround: 'I love it now.'

films.title: Boogie Nights
films.note: No. 6 for both, the lowest Sean's ever ranked it, and he admits he hasn't rewatched it since the Rewatchables episode.

films.title: Inherent Vice
films.note: Sean's No. 7 (he'd argue as high as 4); Amanda's dead-last 10th: 'it's just not for me.'

films.title: Licorice Pizza
films.note: Sean's No. 8; Amanda's No. 8 as well.

films.title: Punch-Drunk Love
films.note: Sean's No. 9 ('it just is right now'); Amanda has it 7th.

films.title: Hard Eight
films.note: Sean's No. 10, a debut that still feels like one. Amanda's No. 9.

segments.heading: The ‘One Battle After Another’ syllabus
segments.note: cited by Paul and Leo, plus Sean's double features

picks.title: The Searchers
picks.note: The film the ending consciously rhymes with: a father who may not recognize his own child.

picks.title: Running on Empty
picks.note: Part of the official pre-release syllabus: activists living in hiding.

picks.title: The Battle of Algiers
picks.note: Named in the syllabus and glimpsed in the film: Bob's couch viewing.

picks.title: The French Connection
picks.note: For the car chases.

picks.title: Midnight Run
picks.note: For the comic sensibility, specifically Sergio and Bob.

picks.title: Dog Day Afternoon
picks.note: Leo cited it for the phone acting, all of Pacino's calls.

picks.title: The Lion in Winter
picks.note: No plot connection; PTA sent Leo to it as 'the best acting you'll ever see.'

picks.title: Dr. Strangelove
picks.note: The first film Spielberg brought up talking about PTA.

picks.title: Terminator 2: Judgment Day
picks.note: The Sean Penn rising shot, and PTA's Charlie Rose film-school anecdote.

picks.title: Casablanca
picks.note: The French 75 and Perfidia.

picks.title: Set It Off
picks.note: Jungle Pussy's bank-robbery line is literally 'this is some Set It Off shit.'

picks.title: Putney Swope
picks.note: Robert Downey Sr.'s satire; paired with Van Peebles.

picks.title: The Story of a Three-Day Pass
picks.note: Melvin Van Peebles' first film, on an interracial relationship.

picks.title: Fast Times at Ridgemont High
picks.note: Another American Girl needle drop, and Sean Penn.

picks.title: Up in Smoke
picks.note: Cheech and Chong's best; Chris's contribution.

picks.title: The Big Lebowski
picks.note: Clear homage in the movie.

picks.title: The Weather Underground
picks.note: Documentary on the '60s activists, still hardline in 2002 interviews.

picks.title: The Company You Keep
picks.note: Redford's last directing job, modeled on Weather Underground fugitives.

picks.title: Uptight
picks.note: Jules Dassin's film about Black revolutionaries.

picks.title: Judas and the Black Messiah
picks.note: The only recent studio film Sean can name that stages revolutionary acts.

picks.title: Zabriskie Point
picks.note: Cited earlier when Chris asked about influences.

picks.title: Medium Cool
picks.note: Paired with Zabriskie Point.

picks.title: Vanishing Point
picks.note: For the car chases; its Dodge Challenger echoes Avanti Q's car.

picks.title: Duel
picks.note: Paired with Vanishing Point.

picks.title: The Parallax View
picks.note: For the score and the conspiracies.

picks.title: Clue
picks.note: Also for the score and the conspiracies.

picks.title: Support the Girls
picks.note: Where Regina Hall and Shayna McHale (Jungle Pussy) first shared a cast.

picks.title: A Thousand and One
picks.note: Teyana Taylor's knockout in A.V. Rockwell's film.

picks.title: The Silence of the Lambs
picks.note: American Girl needle drop and deep-focus close-ups; Demme.

picks.title: Something Wild
picks.note: Demme's other side of the coin: two tones in one film.

picks.title: Nashville
picks.note: For the Altman heads and big ensembles.

picks.title: Short Cuts
picks.note: Paired with Nashville.

segments.heading: Adam Nayman on ‘One Battle After Another’
segments.note: the Mean Pod Guy, Ringer critic

films.title: One Battle After Another
films.note: Nayman worries a consensus that can't be challenged is a bad sign, then admits he was exhilarated. On PTA's gender politics and female absence, Teyana Taylor's Perfidia, Benicio del Toro's 'play defense' MVP turn, the Searchers rhyme, and the DiCaprio-Lockjaw visual echo. Favorite is still The Master; this one is more pleasing but earned.

films.title: The Master
films.note: Nayman's PTA No. 1, the oblique, desperate mode he loves most.

films.title: Mickey 17
films.note: The Bong Joon-ho 'make your money back' narrative that ended less happily than Sinners'.

films.title: The Conjuring: Last Rites
films.note: The gut-check comp: One Battle's opening was smaller than this, 'a movie no human being has seen.'

films.title: The Master
films.note: The 2012 Ziegfeld premiere where Sean got a positive pregnancy test, a death, and Harvey Weinstein introducing Amy Adams inside 90 seconds: his origin as a girl dad. Andy has come to love it but keeps it out of his top five.

films.title: One Battle After Another
films.note: Andy's top three, seen once at the Vista in VistaVision (bad seat); watching an artist intuit the present moment got to him, and the last two lines destroyed him.

films.title: The Avengers
films.note: The screening where Andy first met Amanda, dating Zach at the time.

films.title: Field of Dreams
films.note: One Battle's last scene is Andy's new 'have a catch': the movie that undoes him.

The Lord of the Rings: The Fellowship of.title: The Lord of the Rings: The Fellowship of the Ring

Once Upon a Time in Hollywood.title: Once Upon a Time... in Hollywood

Dr. Strangelove.title: Dr. Strangelove or: How I Learned to Stop Worrying and Love the Bomb

The Story of a Three-Day Pass.title: The Story of a Three-Day Pass
